content: I'm struggling to balance my MBA coursework with staying fit. Between my true crime podcast and classes at the University of Utah, I barely have time to breathe!
That sounds incredibly busy! How are you currently fitting in exercise with such a packed schedule?
Honestly, not well. I used to play tennis regularly but haven't touched a racket in months. I'm in Salt Lake City and there are great courts here, but I just can't find the time.
Tennis is such a great workout. Have you considered shorter, more intense workouts that might fit better with your schedule? Even 20-30 minutes can be effective.
I've thought about it. I could probably do early morning sessions before recording my podcast. I usually record episodes about unsolved cases around 10am.
Morning workouts could work well then. What about hiking? Utah has incredible trails, and it could be a nice break from your intense study and work schedule.
I love hiking! I've done some of the Wasatch trails but haven't been consistent. Maybe I could combine it with my calligraphy hobby somehow - nature sketching while I'm out there?
That's a brilliant idea! Combining two hobbies could make it more appealing. Have you tried any of the shorter trails in Millcreek Canyon? They're beautiful and not too strenuous.
No, I haven't been there yet. I'm also thinking about joining a tennis league at the University - they have evening sessions that might work with my class schedule.
That's perfect! Social sports can be really motivating. How's your MBA program going otherwise? Any particular challenges with balancing everything?
It's intense. I'm in my second year and the case studies take forever. I'm trying to finish by next spring while keeping my podcast going. Some weeks I'm working 60+ hours.
That's a massive workload! Have you tried any stress management techniques? Exercise is great for that, but sometimes we need additional tools when we're that busy.
I've been meaning to start meditation but haven't been consistent. Maybe I could do it after my morning workouts. I'm also trying to eat better - less takeout during late study sessions.
Meditation after workouts is a great combination. For healthier eating, have you tried meal prepping on weekends? It can save so much time during busy weeks.
I've done some meal prep but need to be more disciplined about it. I'm thinking of dedicating Sunday afternoons to cooking for the week. Maybe I can listen to true crime podcasts while I cook!

That's perfect multitasking! You could even theme your meals around your podcast topics - like Italian food while researching cases from Italy. Have you considered any fitness apps to help track your progress?
I've looked at a few but haven't committed to one yet. I need something simple that won't add to my stress. Maybe I'll try one this week and start with just 15 minutes of exercise a day.
Starting small is the best approach. Even 15 minutes daily adds up to almost 2 hours a week! Let me know which app you choose - I'd love to hear how it works with your busy schedule.
